title: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe (Pollo en Mole)
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce with our easy, step-by-step recipe. Rich, flavorful, and perfect for a Mexican feast!
longTailKeywords: how to make chicken mole sauce from scratch
seo.h1: Authentic Chicken Mole Sauce (Pollo en Mole) Recipe
h2Headings: Tips for the Best Chicken Mole Sauce
Serving Suggestions for Pollo en Mole
seo.imageAlt: A plate of authentic chicken mole sauce (Pollo en Mole) with rice and tortillas, garnished with sesame seeds.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, complex flavors of homemade authentic Chicken Mole Sauce, a true Mexican culinary delight.

description: Pollo en Mole is a quintessential Mexican dish featuring tender chicken bathed in a rich, complex, and aromatic mole sauce. This traditional recipe balances a symphony of flavors including chilies, spices, nuts, seeds, and a hint of chocolate, creating an unforgettable culinary experience. It's a celebratory meal that truly showcases the depth and heritage of Mexican gastronomy.

instructions.title: Prepare and Rehydrate Chilies
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Briefly toast the dried ancho, mulato, and pasilla chilies for 30 seconds per side until fragrant; be careful not to burn them. Transfer the toasted chilies to a bowl, cover with hot water, and let them rehydrate for 20 minutes until soft. Drain and reserve the soaking liquid.

instructions.title: Toast Aromatics, Nuts, and Spices
instructions.text: In the same skillet, add the halved tomatoes, chopped onion, and garlic. Cook until softened and lightly charred, about 5-7 minutes. Remove and set aside. Then, add the almonds, peanuts, and sesame seeds to the dry skillet. Toast over medium-low heat until fragrant and lightly browned, about 3-5 minutes. Add the cinnamon stick, cloves, peppercorns, cumin seeds, and oregano, toasting for another minute until aromatic. Be careful not to burn any ingredients.

instructions.title: Blend the Mole Paste
instructions.text: Combine the rehydrated chilies, toasted tomatoes, onion, garlic, nuts, seeds, spices, and the 2 stale corn tortillas or toasted bread into a blender. Add 2 cups of chicken broth. Blend until a very smooth paste forms. You may need to add a little more broth or reserved chili soaking liquid to achieve a smooth consistency. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to remove any solids, pressing with a spoon.

instructions.title: Cook the Mole Sauce
instructions.text: Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Pour the strained mole paste into the hot oil. Stir constantly for 10-15 minutes until the sauce thickens and darkens in color, and the oil separates slightly from the paste. This step is crucial for developing the deep flavors of the mole.

instructions.title: Simmer the Mole with Chocolate
instructions.text: Once the mole paste has cooked down, gradually stir in the remaining 2 cups of chicken broth. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer. Add the Mexican chocolate and sugar. Stir until the chocolate is fully melted and incorporated. Season with salt to taste. Continue to simmer the mole sauce on low heat, stirring occasionally, for at least 20 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken to your desired consistency. If it becomes too thick, add a little more broth.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken and Serve
instructions.text: While the mole simmers, cook the chicken thighs. You can pan-fry, bake, or poach them until fully cooked and tender. Once cooked, add the chicken pieces directly into the simmering mole sauce and let them cook together for another 10-15 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the mole. Serve hot, garnished with a sprinkle of sesame seeds, alongside Mexican rice and warm tortillas.

tips: Toasting the chilies, nuts, and spices properly is key to developing the deep, complex flavors of authentic mole. Be careful not to burn them, as this will make the sauce bitter.

For a deeper flavor, use unsweetened Mexican chocolate. Adjust the sugar content to your preference. If you can't find Mexican chocolate, use a high-quality dark chocolate (70% cacao) and add a pinch of cinnamon.

Mole sauce tastes even better the next day! Prepare it in advance and gently reheat before serving. It can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days or frozen for up to 3 months.
article:
Pollo en Mole, or Chicken Mole, is more than just a dish; it's a culinary journey, a testament to the rich and complex flavors of Mexican cuisine. Often considered the national dish of Mexico, mole is a sauce renowned for its depth, its intricate blend of sweet, savory, spicy, and earthy notes, and its often surprising inclusion of chocolate. If you've ever wanted to delve into authentic Mexican cooking, mastering a classic mole is an incredibly rewarding experience. This article will guide you through creating a spectacular Pollo en Mole, allowing you to bring the vibrant flavors of Mexico right into your kitchen. Prepare to embark on making this incredible Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe.

## What is Chicken Mole Sauce?
At its heart, mole (pronounced MOH-lay) is a generic term for a variety of sauces, many of which are quite complex and labor-intensive to prepare. There are literally hundreds of types of mole across Mexico, varying by region, family tradition, and available ingredients. However, when most people think of "mole," they are often thinking of Mole Poblano, a dark, rich sauce that originated in Puebla, Mexico.

Mole Poblano is characterized by its deep reddish-brown color and a complex flavor profile derived from dozens of ingredients, including several types of dried chiles, nuts, seeds, spices, fruits, vegetables, and, famously, a touch of chocolate. The chocolate isn’t meant to make the sauce sweet, but rather to deepen the color and add a subtle bitterness and richness that balances the other strong flavors. The result is a velvety, multi-layered sauce that perfectly complements chicken, turkey, or pork. Understanding the essence of this sauce is key to appreciating and executing the focus of our Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe today.

## Ingredients for Pollo en Mole
Creating an authentic Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe requires a commitment to sourcing quality ingredients. While the list might seem long, each component plays a crucial role in building the sauce's legendary depth.

For the Chicken:
*   3-4 lbs bone-in, skin-on chicken pieces (thighs, drumsticks, breasts), or a whole chicken cut into pieces
*   1 large onion, quartered
*   3-4 cloves garlic, smashed
*   1 bay leaf
*   Salt to taste

For the Mole Sauce:
*   4 Ancho chiles, dried
*   3 Mulato chiles, dried
*   2 Pasilla chiles, dried
*   1/4 cup vegetable oil (divided)
*   1/4 cup raw almonds
*   1/4 cup pumpkin seeds (pepitas)
*   2 tablespoons sesame seeds, plus more for garnish
*   1/4 cup raisins
*   1/2 small onion, roughly chopped
*   3 cloves garlic, peeled
*   2 ripe Roma tomatoes, roughly chopped
*   1 small plantain, peeled and sliced (or 1/2 banana)
*   1 stale tortilla or 1 slice of bread, torn
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1/4 teaspoon ground cloves
*   1/4 teaspoon ground anise seed
*   Pinch of dried oregano
*   Pinch of dried thyme
*   1 ounce unsweetened Mexican chocolate (or high-quality dark chocolate, 70% or higher cacao)
*   4-6 cups chicken broth (from cooking the chicken)
*   Salt to taste
*   Sugar to taste (optional, to balance flavors)

This comprehensive list covers all the essentials for a truly authentic Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Chicken Mole
Making mole is a labor of love, but each step builds layers of flavor that are incredibly rewarding. Follow this comprehensive Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe carefully:

1.  **Cook the Chicken:** Place the chicken pieces in a large pot with the quartered onion, smashed garlic, bay leaf, and a generous pinch of salt. Cover with water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 30-40 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender. Remove chicken from the broth, reserving the broth. Strain the broth and set aside. Once cooled, you can remove the skin and bones from the chicken for easier serving, if desired.
2.  **Prepare the Chiles:** Remove stems and seeds from the dried Ancho, Mulato, and Pasilla chiles. Heat a dry skillet over medium-low heat and toast the chiles for 30 seconds to 1 minute per side, just until fragrant – be careful not to burn them, or they will become bitter. Place the toasted chiles in a bowl, cover with hot water, and let them rehydrate for 20-30 minutes until soft. Drain, reserving a little soaking liquid.
3.  **Toast Nuts, Seeds, and Spices:** In a clean skillet over medium heat, add 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil. Fry the almonds, pumpkin seeds, and 1 tablespoon of sesame seeds until golden and fragrant. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside. In the same skillet, briefly toast the raisins until plump, then remove.
4.  **Sauté Aromatics and Solids:** Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil to the skillet. Sauté the chopped onion and garlic until softened and lightly browned, about 5-7 minutes. Add the chopped tomatoes, plantain (or banana), and the torn tortilla or bread. Cook for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until everything is soft and slightly caramelized.
5.  **Blend the Mole Base:** In a blender, combine the rehydrated chiles (with a splash of their soaking liquid if needed), the toasted nuts, seeds, raisins, sautéed aromatics, and the spices (cinnamon, cloves, anise, oregano, thyme). Add 2 cups of the reserved chicken broth. Blend until very smooth. This might take several minutes, and you may need to do it in batches, adding more broth as necessary to achieve a smooth, thick paste. Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a large pot, pressing down on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard any remaining solids.
6.  **Simmer the Sauce:** Heat the strained mole sauce in the large pot over medium heat. Bring it to a gentle simmer, stirring frequently to prevent sticking. Continue to simmer for at least 30-45 minutes, or even longer (up to 1.5 hours), stirring often. This simmering process is crucial for the flavors to meld and deepen, and for the sauce to thicken. Add more chicken broth as needed to reach your desired consistency – it should be thick enough to coat the back of a spoon.
7.  **Add Chocolate and Season:** Once the sauce has simmered and thickened, add the unsweetened Mexican chocolate. Stir constantly until the chocolate is fully melted and incorporated. Season the mole generously with salt. Taste and adjust the seasoning; you might want a pinch of sugar to balance the bitterness of the chocolate and the heat of the chiles, or more salt to enhance the overall flavor.
8.  **Combine with Chicken:** Gently add the cooked chicken pieces to the mole sauce. Let it simmer together for another 15-20 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the mole.

## Tips for the Best Chicken Mole Sauce
Mastering this Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe can elevate your cooking to new heights. Here are some tips for achieving the best results:

*   **Don't Rush the Toasting:** Each ingredient needs careful toasting or frying to release its full aroma and flavor. Burnt chiles or nuts will make your mole bitter.
*   **Balance is Key:** Mole is all about balance. Taste frequently throughout the simmering process. Adjust salt, sugar, and even a tiny bit of lime juice if needed to brighten the flavors.
*   **Quality Chocolate Matters:** Use unsweetened Mexican chocolate if possible, or a high-quality dark chocolate (70% cacao or higher). Avoid milk chocolate, which will make the sauce too sweet and cloying.
*   **Patience with Simmering:** The long simmer time is not optional. It allows the complex flavors to meld and develop into that signature deep mole taste.
*   **Make Ahead:** Mole actually tastes better the next day, after the flavors have had more time to marry. Reheat gently, adding a little more broth if it's too thick.
*   **Adjust Spice Level:** For a milder mole, use fewer Ancho or Pasilla chiles and ensure all seeds are removed. For more heat, add a arbol chile or two to the mix.

## Serving Suggestions for Pollo en Mole
This fantastic Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe pairs beautifully with simple accompaniments that allow its complex flavors to shine. Serve Pollo en Mole with:

*   **Warm Corn Tortillas:** Essential for scooping up every last drop of the rich sauce.
*   **Mexican Rice:** A classic side that complements the mole perfectly.
*   **Refried Beans:** Another traditional and comforting accompaniment.
*   **Garnish:** A sprinkle of fresh cilantro, a dollop of Mexican crema, and a dusting of toasted sesame seeds add both flavor and visual appeal.
*   **Queso Fresco:** Crumbled over the top for a fresh, salty contrast.

## Variations & Substitutions for Mole
While our Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe focuses on a traditional Poblano style, the beauty of mole lies in its versatility.

*   **Different Meats:** Mole is excellent with turkey (especially for holidays), pork, or even beef. For a vegetarian option, try it with roasted vegetables like sweet potatoes, cauliflower, or hearty mushrooms.
*   **Chile Combinations:** Experiment with different dried chiles. For example, adding a few Chipotles Moritas can introduce a smoky heat. Mole Verde uses fresh green chiles, tomatillos, and herbs for a brighter, tangier sauce.
*   **Nut Alternatives:** If you have nut allergies, you can omit the almonds and pumpkin seeds, or substitute with sunflower seeds (though the flavor profile will change slightly).
*   **Sweetness Adjustments:** Some recipes call for a small amount of piloncillo (Mexican brown sugar cone) or dark brown sugar instead of white sugar to balance the bitterness.
*   **Thickeners:** While the traditional method relies on the chiles, nuts, and toasted bread/tortilla for thickening, some recipes might use a little masa harina (corn flour) at the end if the sauce isn't thick enough.

Creating Pollo en Mole is a deeply satisfying culinary experience. It’s a dish that tells a story of history, culture, and incredible flavor. While it requires time and patience, the reward is a truly unforgettable meal that will impress your family and friends. Don't be intimidated by the ingredient list or the process; embrace the journey, and you'll have mastered a truly unforgettable Chicken Mole Sauce Recipe.
